name: Seinfeld Broadcast Email — Single Email-About-Nothing Copy Framework

sections: DotCom Secrets Secret #8 — Daily Seinfeld Sequence (worked examples: '$20 million down the toilet', 'Jiu Jitsu')
Copywriting Secrets — 3 Sales Formulas (PAS / If-you-want-X / Before-After-Bridge), headline families, Ultimate Bullet Formula, Stealth/Columbo close, Ten Reasons People Buy

trigger.primary: Authoring unit — invoked whenever any broadcast (the daily Seinfeld engine, the storyline rotation, a 3-closes campaign, or a win-back send) needs ONE email body.
trigger.trigger_type_ghl: Compose Email Campaign for segment 'seinfeld-broadcast'  (or a Send-Email step inside a workflow)

sequence.purpose: Get the open; carry curiosity and an open loop.
sequence.subjectAngle: Headline families (Edwards): '[True Story]…', '[X] is like [unexpected Y]', Warning, How-To-with-a-twist, the 'even if' objection-killer. Keep it about THEM, not you.

sequence.purpose: Suck the reader into the story fast.
sequence.bodyFramework: Open in the middle of an entertaining moment at the point of high drama or absurdity (book: 'always start any good story at the point of high drama').

The episode itself — embarrassing / holiday / vacation / regret purchase / beloved purchase / rage->laughter / kid-dog antics / funny past lesson. One-two sentences per line, white space, personality, don't fear small grammar slips.

sequence.purpose: Bridge entertainment to the sale without a hard sell.
sequence.bodyFramework: 'So, why do I tell you this?...' — connect the story's lesson to the offer using 'which means...' + at least one of the Ten Reasons People Buy.
Tie to core offer / other product / affiliate; ONE link (UTM-tagged). Honest reason to act if any urgency is claimed.

sequence.purpose: Reinforce personality; second-chance read for skimmers.
sequence.bodyFramework: Add a takeaway, a flaw, or an honest reason to act (book P.S. example: 'no emailing me telling me it's not healthy').

bodyFrameworkVariants.PAS: Problem / Agitate / Solve — for problem-aware tie-ins (cold-ish).
bodyFrameworkVariants.IfYouWantX: 'If you want X, do this' — for warm tie-ins.
bodyFrameworkVariants.BeforeAfterBridge: Before / After / Bridge with future pacing — for transformation tie-ins.

copyPersona.secondary: Jim Edwards / Copywriting Secrets — subject-line families, Ultimate Bullet Formula ('it ___ so you can ___ which means ___'), Stealth/Columbo close, pronoun audit, every claim provable.

ghlBuild.buildType: Reusable Email Template, not a workflow.
ghlBuild.location: Marketing > Emails > Templates — saved as 'Seinfeld Broadcast Shell'.
ghlBuild.structure: Merge field {{contact.first_name}}; plain-text-feel design (scannable, white space, no heavy template chrome); single CTA button -> offer URL with UTM; P.S. block.
ghlBuild.notes: Keep design minimal so it renders like a personal email (book aesthetic). One CTA only — never compete CTAs.

faithfulness: Hook-Story-Offer, high-drama open, 90/10 ratio, formatting rules, and the example subject lines/P.S. are from DotCom Secrets Secret #8. The 3 email formulas, headline families, Ultimate Bullet Formula, Stealth/Columbo close, and Ten Reasons People Buy are from Copywriting Secrets. GHL template mechanics are the Skill-44 mapping.
